questions.text: How does Allah describe the origin of the Quran in the opening of this Surah?
questions.answer: It is the revelation of the Book about which there is no doubt, from the Lord of the worlds.

questions.text: What is the response to the claim that the Prophet invented the Quran?
questions.answer: It is the truth from your Lord to warn a people to whom no warner has come before, so that they may be guided.

questions.text: In how many days did Allah create the heavens and the earth?
questions.answer: Six days, and then He established Himself above the Throne.

questions.text: Can anyone besides Allah protect or intercede for people?
questions.answer: No, mankind has no protector or intercessor besides Him.

questions.text: How does Allah manage the affairs of the universe from the heaven to the earth?
questions.answer: He directs every affair, and then it ascends to Him in a Day whose length is a thousand years of those which you count.

questions.text: What attributes of Allah are mentioned in the context of His knowledge of the universe?
questions.answer: He is the Knower of the unseen and the witnessed, the Exalted in Might, the Merciful.

questions.text: How did Allah begin the creation of man from the earth?
questions.answer: He began the creation of man from clay.

questions.text: From what substance did Allah make man's offspring?
questions.answer: From an extract of a humble fluid (semen).

questions.text: What did Allah do after fashioning the human body and breathing life into it?
questions.answer: He gave you hearing, sight, and hearts (intellect), yet little are you grateful.

questions.text: What is the skeptical question of the disbelievers regarding resurrection?
questions.answer: They say, 'When we are lost within the earth, will we indeed be created anew?'

questions.text: What is the 'root' reason for the disbelievers' rejection of resurrection?
questions.answer: They are in disbelief regarding the meeting with their Lord.

questions.text: Who is specifically mentioned as being in charge of taking souls at death?
questions.answer: The Angel of Death who has been entrusted with you.

questions.text: How will the criminals look before their Lord on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: They will have their heads lowered in shame, saying, 'Our Lord, we have seen and heard, so return us to the world so we may do righteousness.'

questions.text: Could Allah have given every soul its guidance if He had willed?
questions.answer: Yes, but His word will be fulfilled that He will fill Hell with Jinn and men together.

questions.text: Why will the inhabitants of Hell be told to 'taste' the punishment?
questions.answer: Because they forgot the meeting of that Day, so Allah has forgotten them; they taste the punishment for what they used to do.

questions.text: Who are the only ones who truly believe in Allah's verses?
questions.answer: Those who, when reminded of them, fall down in prostration and exalt Allah with praise, and they are not arrogant.

questions.text: What is the characteristic of believers regarding their sleep at night?
questions.answer: They forsake their beds to call upon their Lord in fear and hope.

questions.text: What do the believers do with the provision Allah has given them?
questions.answer: They spend from it in charity and good causes.

questions.text: Does anyone know the specific reward hidden for the righteous in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: No soul knows what has been hidden for them of comfort for the eyes (supreme joy) as a reward for what they used to do.

questions.text: Is the believer equal to the defiantly disobedient (Fasiq)?
questions.answer: No, they are not equal.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who believe and do righteous deeds?
questions.answer: Gardens of Refuge as a hospitality for what they used to do.

questions.text: What is the refuge for the defiantly disobedient?
questions.answer: The Fire; every time they wish to get out, they are returned to it and told to taste the punishment they used to deny.

questions.text: What is the 'lesser punishment' mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The trials and calamities of this world, which come before the greater punishment of the Hereafter, so that people may return to the right path.

questions.text: Who is more unjust than one who is reminded of Allah's verses and then turns away?
questions.answer: No one; Allah will surely take retribution from the criminals.

questions.text: Which Prophet was given the Scripture (Torah) before Muhammad (SAW)?
questions.answer: Prophet Musa (AS).

questions.text: What was the purpose of giving the Scripture to Musa?
questions.answer: As a guidance for the Children of Israel.

questions.text: What were the two qualities of the leaders among the Children of Israel?
questions.answer: They were patient and they had certain faith in Allah's signs.

questions.text: Who will judge between people on the Day of Resurrection regarding their differences?
questions.answer: Allah, your Lord.

questions.text: Is it not a guidance for people to see how many generations Allah destroyed before them?
questions.answer: Yes, they walk among their dwellings; indeed in that are signs, if they would only listen.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the earth's vegetation as a sign of His power?
questions.answer: He drives water to barren land and brings forth crops from which their livestock and they themselves eat.

questions.text: What is the skeptical question the disbelievers ask regarding the 'Day of Decision'?
questions.answer: They ask, 'When will this decision be, if you are truthful?'

questions.text: Will the faith of disbelievers benefit them on the Day of Decision?
questions.answer: No, their faith will not benefit them then, nor will they be given a delay.

questions.text: What is the final instruction to the Prophet at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: Turn away from them and wait; indeed, they are also waiting.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'As-Sajdah'?
questions.answer: The Prostration.

questions.text: Why is prostration the central theme of this Surah?
questions.answer: Because it distinguishes the true believers, who humble themselves before Allah, from the arrogant criminals.

questions.text: What is the 'Ruh' that was breathed into man?
questions.answer: The soul or life-force created by Allah that gives life and consciousness.

questions.text: What is the 'Lord of the worlds' (Rabb al-Alamin)?
questions.answer: The Creator, Sustainer, and Governor of all that exists.

questions.text: What is the 'Night Prayer' (Tahajjud) mentioned implicitly?
questions.answer: Arising from bed to worship Allah when others are asleep.

questions.text: What is the 'Comfort for the eyes' (Qurrata A'yun)?
questions.answer: The ultimate satisfaction and joy found in the rewards of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Decision' (Yawm al-Fath)?
questions.answer: The Day of Judgment when the final victory of truth over falsehood is manifest.

questions.text: What is the 'Humble fluid' (Ma'in Mahin)?
questions.answer: A reference to the biological origin of human reproduction, showing man's humble beginnings.

questions.text: What is the 'Barren land' (Ard al-Juruuz)?
questions.answer: Dry land with no vegetation, which Allah brings to life with rain.

questions.text: Why does Allah mention the destruction of previous generations?
questions.answer: As a historical proof and a warning for people to learn from the mistakes of the past.

questions.text: What is the 'Fear and Hope' (Khawfan wa Tama'a)?
questions.answer: The balanced emotional state of a believer when calling upon Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Greater Punishment' (Al-Adhab al-Akbar)?
questions.answer: The eternal punishment of the Hereafter for those who reject the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Certainty' (Yaqin) of the leaders of Israel?
questions.answer: Their unshakeable belief in the signs and promises of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Hospitality' (Nuzulan)?
questions.answer: The generous and honorable reception believers will receive in Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'Retribution' (Muntaqimun)?
questions.answer: Allah's divine justice against those who knowingly commit crimes and reject His signs.

questions.text: What is the 'Warning' role of the Quran mentioned in the beginning?
questions.answer: To alert people to the consequences of their choices and guide them to safety.

questions.text: What is the final lesson of As-Sajdah?
questions.answer: That true success lies in humility, prayer, and patience, while arrogance and denial lead to eternal regret.

questions.text: What are the disjointed letters that open this Surah?
questions.answer: Alif, Lam, Meem.

questions.text: What is the 'Throne' (Al-Arsh) mentioned in the creation passage?
questions.answer: The majestic seat of Allah's authority over the entire universe.

questions.text: What is the 'management of affairs' from heaven to earth?
questions.answer: Allah's constant and perfect control over every detail of existence.

questions.text: What is the 'witnessed' (Shahadah)?
questions.answer: The physical world that we can see and perceive.

questions.text: What is the 'unseen' (Ghayb)?
questions.answer: The hidden reality of the universe that only Allah fully knows.

questions.text: What is the 'Clay' (Tin) origin of man?
questions.answer: A reminder of the physical and humble components of the human body.

questions.text: What is the 'Angel of Death' (Malak al-Mawt)?
questions.answer: The angel assigned by Allah to separate the soul from the body.

questions.text: What is the 'Despair' of the criminals on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: Their realization that their denial was wrong and it is too late to change.

questions.text: What is the 'Forgetting' of Allah toward the disbelievers?
questions.answer: His withdrawal of mercy and attention from them as they ignored His message.

questions.text: What is the 'Forsaking of beds'?
questions.answer: Preferring the worship of Allah over the comfort of sleep.

questions.text: What is the 'Spending' (Yunfiqun)?
questions.answer: Sharing one's resources with others for the sake of Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Garden of Refuge' (Jannat al-Ma'wa)?
questions.answer: A specific, beautiful part of Paradise for the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'Multiplied reward' for righteousness?
questions.answer: Allah giving back more than what was spent or done in His way.

questions.text: What is the 'Truth' which the Quran is?
questions.answer: The ultimate and final revelation from the Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Warner' (Nadhir)?
questions.answer: A messenger who cautions people about the dangers of their actions.

questions.text: What is the 'Enlightenment' of the hearts?
questions.answer: The spiritual clarity gained through faith and the Quran.

questions.text: What is the 'Specified term' of the heavens and earth?
questions.answer: The duration for which the current universe will exist.

questions.text: What is the 'Ascension' of affairs to Allah?
questions.answer: The return of all information and actions to the Creator for His final decision.

questions.text: What is the 'Exalted in Might' (Al-Aziz)?
questions.answer: Allah's supreme and irresistible power.

questions.text: What is the 'Merciful' (Ar-Rahim)?
questions.answer: Allah's encompassing kindness and compassion toward His creation.

questions.text: What is the 'Prostration' (Sajdah) an act of?
questions.answer: Total submission, humility, and worship.

questions.text: What is the 'Arrogance' (Istikbar) warned against?
questions.answer: The refusal to accept the truth and submit to the Creator.

questions.text: What is the 'Call' (Dua) of the believers at night?
questions.answer: Their private and sincere supplication to Allah.

questions.text: What is the 'Hospitality' of Paradise?
questions.answer: The welcome and honors given to the righteous.

questions.text: What is the 'Fire' (An-Nar)?
questions.answer: The place of eternal punishment for the ungrateful.

questions.text: What is the 'Lesser Adhab'?
questions.answer: Worldly trials meant to guide people back to the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'Greater Adhab'?
questions.answer: The severe and eternal punishment of the Hereafter.

questions.text: What is the 'Day of Decision'?
questions.answer: The time when every soul's fate is finally and irrevocably determined.

questions.text: What is the 'Wait' (Intizar)?
questions.answer: The period before the final judgment where both sides expect their outcome.

questions.text: What is the 'Promise of Allah'?
questions.answer: His guarantee that truth will prevail and justice will be done.
